images.alt:
Highly detailed steampunk fantasy scene of three fierce female pirates aboard a massive steampunk airship sailing through stormy skies above the clouds. Each pirate has a distinct appearance and personality: one dark-skinned woman with silver dreadlocks and glowing amber goggles, one pale redhead with wild curly hair and mechanical arm enhancements, and one East Asian woman with long black hair streaked with blue and intricate brass tattoos. All wear rugged leather corsets, layered belts, buckles, fingerless gloves, high boots, and weathered pirate coats with brass gears and steam-powered accessories. Elegant but dangerous swords hang at their hips, some with glowing steam-tech details.
The airship deck is filled with giant gears, pipes, steam vents, spinning propellers, hanging lanterns, ropes, and brass machinery. One pirate stands confidently at the wheel, another leans against a cannon with a smirk, while the third watches the horizon with her sword drawn. Billowing steam, dramatic cinematic clouds, warm golden lighting mixed with cold storm atmosphere, fluttering flags, wind blowing hair and coats dynamically.
Ultra-detailed steampunk aesthetic, cinematic composition, realistic textures, intricate leather and metalwork, fantasy adventure mood, dramatic lighting, highly expressive faces, dynamic poses, volumetric fog, painterly realism, epic sky backdrop, rich bronze and copper color palette, masterpiece quality, fantasy realism.

Go Forward:
Write prompts like instructions, not spells. Test, adjust, and stop chasing magic words.

record.bridgyOriginalText:
The Myth:
There's a universal prompt template that works every time for every task, person, or problem.

The Truth:
Every prompt depends on your goal, your context, and your audience. A prompt that nails a marketing headline flops when debugging code. Copying someone else's perfect prompt ignores your unique situation. Even AI doesn't judge ethics the same way humans do. It mirrors data, not moral truth. You can't paste a template and expect wisdom. (1/2)

images.alt: The visual is a gritty, photorealistic snapshot of a Montreal backyard evening, circa late 90s. The setting is a cramped, wooden deck attached to a red brick triplex. I am sitting in a mismatched plastic lawn chair, wearing an authentic, vintage Montreal Canadiens jersey—red, white, and blue—and slightly baggy jeans. I have a sweating bottle of beer in one hand, resting on my knee. The other hand is fiddling with a battered, silver transistor radio sitting on a small plastic table next to an overflowing ashtray. My expression is relaxed but distracted, looking off into the distance where the city lights are just starting to blur. The lighting is warm and golden, coming from a string of bare bulbs hanging above the deck, but the shadows are long and deep. The texture is grainy, like film shot at a high ISO. The vibe is intimate and melancholic—a moment of pause in the chaos of the city life.

images.alt: The visual is a mind-bending, hyper-realistic fusion of deep-sea mystery and the vastness of outer space. The setting is the cosmos, but instead of a vacuum, it's filled with bioluminescent, deep-sea darkness. I am floating weightlessly, swimming forward, reaching out with one hand. I am wearing a sleek, iridescent wetsuit that shimmers like the surface of a bubble, reflecting the starlight. My hair is floating around my face like seaweed. Directly in front of me is a massive Beluga whale, its skin translucent and glowing faintly with internal starlight, looking like a creature made of nebulas. It is facing me, its dark eye gazing into my soul with ancient wisdom. Surrounding us are floating coral reefs made of crystal and star clusters, with jellyfish that look like exploding supernovas drifting by. The lighting is ethereal, coming from the whale and the distant galaxies, casting soft, otherworldly blue and violet glows on my face. The vibe is transcendent, silent, and overwhelmingly beautiful—like two small souls meeting in an infinite universe.

9では3以上に覚えるのが遅く、11ではイオナズンやバギクロスやライデインに劣るという6とは別の意味でもっとも不遇に…最上位のメラガイアーは強いのにねえ。リメイク版もちょっと触れると1HD-2Dではなんと竜王専用呪文に、3HD-2Dは覚えるの早くなったお陰で結構使える呪文に、5リメイクは山彦の帽子がまさかの削除で今一歩に。8の3DS版はスカウトモンスターが使うメラゾーマが改善されました。ゲーム以外ではダイの大冒険の老バーン様が使うカイザーフェニックスがもっとも有名かと思います

ゲームの呪文・特技
No52　メラゾーマ（ドラクエシリーズ）
3で初登場した8まではメラ系最強呪文でボスに対して有力なダメージソースになる…と思いきや…。まず3では覚えるのが遅すぎて活躍の機会が少なく4ではボスにメラ系耐性持ちが増えて不遇だったりします。活躍するのは5でミルドラースやエスタークに対して通りがよくビアンカやフローラで火力を出したいならやまびこメラゾーマです。もっとも不遇なのが6でこともあろうにポンコツ職の魔法戦士でしか覚えないのがなんとも…。7でもアルテマソードの存在がメラゾーマの存在価値がなく、8も双龍撃ちがあるけど7よりはマシかな(返信に続く)
